rationale: The citing editorial uses Grewal, Gupta, and Hamilton (2020) multiple times to provide concrete empirical support for central claims about JMR's cross-domain scope and methodological diversity. It cites specific findings from that paper (e.g., roughly one-third of JMR articles using multiple methods, representation of behavioral/quantitative/strategy domains) to justify the new editorial agenda on integration and triangulation. This is more than a passing literature mention because the cited paper supplies key evidence underlying the citing paper's problem framing and policy direction.
relation_description: Hamilton (2024) relies on Grewal et al. (2020) as core background evidence that JMR already spans domains and methods. Those findings are used to motivate and legitimize the editorial's proposed priorities for cross-domain integration, methodological triangulation, and journal policy.
